g.data-narration: Welcome to Introduction to Data Structures. We'll explore fundamental concepts, classifications, and practical applications for software development and competitive programming.
A data structure organizes and stores data enabling efficient access and modification. It's fundamental to algorithm efficiency and software design.
g.text: A way of organizing and storing data that enables efficient access and modification.
Fundamental to algorithm efficiency and software design applications.

g.data-narration: Data structures are classified into linear (sequential elements) and non-linear (hierarchical relationships). Linear structures include arrays, linked lists, stacks, and queues.
g.text: Linear: Elements arranged sequentially (Arrays, Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues)

g.data-narration: An ADT defines data by its behavior without implementation details. It provides abstraction, separating interface from how operations work internally in code.
g.text: Mathematical model defining data by its behavior (operations) without implementation details.
Provides abstraction by separating interface from implementation.

g.data-narration: Time complexity measures algorithm runtime using Big O notation. Space complexity measures memory required. Both are essential for analyzing algorithm efficiency and optimization.
g.text: Time: Algorithm runtime as function of input size using Big O notation
Space: Memory required as function of input size
O(1) O(log n) O(n) O(n log n) O(n²)

g.data-narration: Data structures support key operations: traversal, insertion, deletion, search, sorting, and merging. Each structure optimizes different operations for specific use cases.
g.text: Core operations: Traversal, Insertion, Deletion, Search, Sorting, Merging
Each structure optimizes different operations for specific use cases.
Visit all → Add → Remove → Find → Arrange → Combine
Fundamental operations all structures must support

g.data-narration: Stack follows LIFO principle, Queue follows FIFO. These fundamental ADTs are used in function calls, expression evaluation, and job scheduling in operating systems.
g.text: Stack: LIFO - Last In First Out (push/pop operations)
Queue: FIFO - First In First Out (enqueue/dequeue operations)
